shot.notes: Synthetic educational data only. Regular Type-I ELMs in an H-mode edge. Not real measurements.

elmAnalyses.label: ELM analysis on D-alpha recycling light
assumptions: Crashes appear as sharp positive spikes in the source signal.
Crash spacing reflects the ELM cycle.
limitations: Threshold-based detection can miss small crashes.
Classification is a coarse heuristic, not a pedestal-stability calculation.

claims.statement: Regular Type-I ELMs at ~100 Hz pace the H-mode edge, each shedding a fraction of the pedestal stored energy.

evidence.rationale: Ten evenly spaced D-alpha crashes (regularity 0.98) give an ELM frequency near 100 Hz.
Stored energy drops step-wise at each crash, consistent with ELM pedestal losses.
The pre-ELM phase alone does not distinguish ELM type.
